claim: OpenClaw should be treated as one launch/router surface inside a broader SCBE multi-swarm router, not as the whole product boundary.
verified_with: User correction plus Ollama Launch evidence that multiple coding tools can be launched through the same local/cloud model surface.

claim: Ollama Launch supports coding-tool integrations such as Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, and Droid with local or cloud models.
verified_with: Ollama Launch CLI docs search result also lists OpenClaw/Pi style integrations.

claim: The same model can show large performance variance across coding-agent wrappers.
verified_with: SWE-Bench Mobile reports up to 6x performance gap across agents.

claim: Current industrial mobile coding-agent success remains low even for the best tested configurations.
verified_with: Paper abstract reports best configurations at 12% task success.

claim: Skills often provide little benefit unless narrowly matched to task and context.
verified_with: SWE-Skills-Bench reports 39/49 skills with zero pass-rate improvement and average +1.2%.

claim: IDE-style benchmarks emphasize structured tool ecosystems beyond raw terminal execution.
verified_with: IDE-Bench abstract describes structured file editing, code search, testing tools, and full-stack tasks.

claim: Broader coding-agent benchmarks should include test generation, code review fixing, and style fixing, not only bug fixing.
verified_with: OmniCode abstract defines four categories: bug fixing, test generation, code review fixing, style fixing.

claim: Hermes Agent is relevant as a memory/skills/self-improvement reference but native Windows support is early beta.
verified_with: README says Windows native PowerShell support is early beta and WSL2 is more battle-tested.

claim: OpenCode is a terminal-first agent with many providers and OpenAI-compatible endpoint support.
verified_with: Source states MIT terminal agent, provider support, and local Ollama/LM Studio compatibility.
